1.o: That essential Good, that uncreated Excellence, which had been from the beginning, from eternity, as equal with the Father, and which at length appeared in human nature for the salvation of sinners, was the great subject concerning which the apostle wrote to his brethren. The apostles had seen Him while they witnessed his wisdom and holiness, his miracles, and love and mercy, during some years, till they saw him crucified for sinners, and afterwards risen from the dead. They touched him, so as to have full proof of his resurrection. This Divine Person, the Word of life, the Word of God, appeared in human nature, that he might be the Author and Giver of eternal life to mankind, through the redemption of his blood, and the influence of his new-creating Spirit. The apostles declared what they had seen and heard, that believers might share their comforts and everlasting advantages. They had free access to God the Father. They had a happy experience of the truth in their souls, and showed its excellence in their lives. This communion of believers with the Father and the Son, is begun and kept up by the influences of the Holy Spirit. The benefits Christ bestows, are not like the scanty possessions of the world, causing jealousies in others; but the joy and happiness of communion with God is all-sufficient, so that any number may partake of it; and all who are warranted to say, that truly their fellowship is with the Father, will desire to lead others to partake of the same blessedness.
1.m:
The main topic the apostle wrote about was Jesus, who existed forever as an excellent being equal to God the Father. He came to earth in human form to save people from their sins. 

The apostles saw Jesus for several years. They watched His wise actions, holy behavior, miracles, and loving kindness. Then they saw Him die on the cross for our sins. Afterward, they saw Him rise from the dead. They even touched Him to prove He really rose again.

This divine person, called the Word of Life and the Word of God, became human so He could give us eternal life. He did this through His sacrifice and the power of His Spirit. 

The apostles told everyone what they saw and heard. They wanted other believers to enjoy the same blessings. These believers could talk freely to God the Father. They felt the truth in their hearts and lived good lives because of it.

Believers connect with God the Father and Jesus through the Holy Spirit’s work. The gifts Jesus gives aren’t like worldly things that cause jealousy. Instead, the joy of knowing God is enough for everyone. Anyone who truly shares in God’s presence wants to help others join too.

1.o: A message from the Lord Jesus, the Word of life, the eternal Word, we should all gladly receive. The great God should be represented to this dark world, as pure and perfect light. As this is the nature of God, his doctrines and precepts must be such. And as his perfect happiness cannot be separated from his perfect holiness, so our happiness will be in proportion to our being made holy. To walk in darkness, is to live and act against religion. God holds no heavenly fellowship or intercourse with unholy souls. There is no truth in their profession; their practice shows its folly and falsehood. The eternal Life, the eternal Son, put on flesh and blood, and died to wash us from our sins in his own blood, and procures for us the sacred influences by which sin is to be subdued more and more, till it is quite done away. While the necessity of a holy walk is insisted upon, as the effect and evidence of the knowledge of God in Christ Jesus, the opposite error of self-righteous pride is guarded against with equal care. All who walk near to God, in holiness and righteousness, are sensible that their best days and duties are mixed with sin. God has given testimony to the sinfulness of the world, by providing a sufficient, effectual Sacrifice for sin, needed in all ages; and the sinfulness of believers themselves is shown, by requiring them continually to confess their sins, and to apply by faith to the blood of that Sacrifice. Let us plead guilty before God, be humble, and willing to know the worst of our case. Let us honestly confess all our sins in their full extent, relying wholly on his mercy and truth through the righteousness of Christ, for a free and full forgiveness, and our deliverance from the power and practice of sin.
1.m:
We should all happily accept the message from Jesus, who is the Word of life and the eternal Word. God, who is perfect and full of light, should shine brightly in this dark world. His teachings and rules must reflect his purity and perfection. Since God’s happiness depends on his holiness, our happiness will match how much we become holy. Walking in darkness means living against what God wants. God doesn’t have anything to do with unholy people in heaven. People who claim to follow God but don’t really believe show their lies through their actions. 

Jesus, the eternal Son, came to Earth as a human, died to forgive our sins, and gives us strength to overcome sin until it’s gone completely. We need to walk in holiness as proof that we truly know God through Jesus. At the same time, we shouldn't think too highly of ourselves because even those close to God realize their actions aren’t always perfect. 

God showed the world’s sinfulness by giving us a sacrifice that works perfectly to forgive sins at all times. Believers see their own sinfulness by needing to keep asking for forgiveness. We should admit our wrongdoings to God, stay humble, and want to know the worst about ourselves. Be honest about all your sins and trust fully in God’s mercy and truth through Christ’s righteousness for complete forgiveness and freedom from sin.

2.o: When have an Advocate with the Father; one who has undertaken, and is fully able, to plead in behalf of every one who applies for pardon and salvation in his name, depending on his pleading for them. He is “Jesus,” the Saviour, and “Christ,” the Messiah, the Anointed. He alone is “the Righteous One,” who received his nature pure from sin, and as our Surety perfectly obeyed the law of God, and so fulfilled all righteousness. All men, in every land, and through successive generations, are invited to come to God through this all-sufficient atonement, and by this new and living way. The gospel, when rightly understood and received, sets the heart against all sin, and stops the allowed practice of it; at the same time it gives blessed relief to the wounded consciences of those who have sinned.
2.m:
We have someone to speak up for us with God. Jesus Christ is our Savior and the promised Messiah. Only he is truly righteous, having lived without sin and kept all of God’s laws perfectly. 

People everywhere, now and forever, are called to turn to God through Jesus’ perfect sacrifice. When we understand and accept the good news, it helps us hate sin and find peace for our guilty hearts.

2.o: What knowledge of Christ can that be, which sees not that he is most worthy of our entire obedience? And a disobedient life shows there is neither religion nor honesty in the professor. The love of God is perfected in him that keeps his commandments. God's grace in him attains its true mark, and produces its sovereign effect as far as may be in this world, and this is man's regeneration; though never absolutely perfect here. Yet this observing Christ's commands, has holiness and excellency which, if universal, would make the earth resemble heaven itself. The command to love one another had been in force from the beginning of the world; but it might be called a new command as given to Christians. It was new in them, as their situation was new in respect of its motives, rules, and obligations. And those who walk in hatred and enmity to believers, remain in a dark state. Christian love teaches us to value our brother's soul, and to dread every thing hurtful to his purity and peace. Where spiritual darkness dwells, in mind, the judgment, and the conscience will be darkened, and will mistake the way to heavenly life. These things demand serious self-examination; and earnest prayer, that God would show us what we are, and whither we are going.
2.m:
Knowing Christ means seeing that he deserves all our obedience. A life that doesn't follow him shows a lack of both faith and honesty. Loving God fully happens when someone obeys his commands. This obedience shows God’s work in changing a person, bringing about a kind of rebirth. Even if perfection isn’t reached, keeping Christ’s commands brings holiness and excellence. 

If everyone followed these commands perfectly, the world would look more like heaven. Jesus told people to love each other long ago. But for Christians, loving others became a new command because their reasons, rules, and duties were different. People who hate believers stay in darkness. True Christian love values a friend’s soul and protects them from harm. When someone’s mind and conscience are dark, they misunderstand paths to eternal life. We need to examine ourselves carefully and pray earnestly so God can show us who we are and where we’re headed.

2.o: As Christians have their peculiar states, so they have peculiar duties; but there are precepts and obedience common to all, particularly mutual love, and contempt of the world. The youngest sincere disciple is pardoned: the communion of saints is attended with the forgiveness of sins. Those of the longest standing in Christ's school need further advice and instruction. Even fathers must be written unto, and preached unto; none are too old to learn. But especially young men in Christ Jesus, though they are arrived at strength of spirit and sound sense, and have successfully resisted first trials and temptations, breaking off bad habits and connexions, and entered in at the strait gate of true conversion. The different descriptions of Christians are again addressed. Children in Christ know that God is their Father; it is wisdom. Those advanced believers, who know Him that was from the beginning, before this world was made, may well be led thereby to give up this world. It will be the glory of young persons to be strong in Christ, and his grace. By the word of God they overcome the wicked one.
2.m:
Christians have special duties based on where they are in life. However, some rules apply to everyone, such as loving each other and not caring about worldly things. A new believer is forgiven just like an older one. Everyone needs guidance, even those who have been following Jesus for a long time. Older people still need to listen and learn. 

Young Christians, even if they are strong in faith now and have passed early tests, should keep growing. New followers of Christ should remember that God is their Father. People who have known Jesus since the beginning should let that lead them away from the world. Young Christians should grow stronger in Jesus' power. They use God’s Word to defeat evil.

2.o: The things of the world may be desired and possessed for the uses and purposes which God intended, and they are to be used by his grace, and to his glory; but believers must not seek or value them for those purposes to which sin abuses them. The world draws the heart from God; and the more the love of the world prevails, the more the love of God decays. The things of the world are classed according to the three ruling inclinations of depraved nature. 1. The lust of the flesh, of the body: wrong desires of the heart, the appetite of indulging all things that excite and inflame sensual pleasures. 2. The lust of the eyes: the eyes are delighted with riches and rich possessions; this is the lust of covetousness. 3. The pride of life: a vain man craves the grandeur and pomp of a vain-glorious life; this includes thirst after honour and applause. The things of the world quickly fade and die away; desire itself will ere long fail and cease, but holy affection is not like the lust that passes away. The love of God shall never fail. Many vain efforts have been made to evade the force of this passage by limitations, distinctions, or exceptions. Many have tried to show how far we may be carnally-minded, and love the world; but the plain meaning of these verses cannot easily be mistaken. Unless this victory over the world is begun in the heart, a man has no root in himself, but will fall away, or at most remain an unfruitful professor. Yet these vanities are so alluring to the corruption in our hearts, that without constant watching and prayer, we cannot escape the world, or obtain victory over the god and prince of it.
2.m:
The things of the world can be desired and used for the reasons God intended, and they should be used with God’s help and to honor him. But believers shouldn’t seek or value them for sinful purposes. 

The world pulls the heart away from God. As people love the world more, their love for God weakens. 

Things in the world fit into three main categories based on human nature: 
1. The lust of the flesh—wrong desires that lead to indulgence in physical pleasures.
2. The lust of the eyes—desires for wealth and material possessions, which is greed.
3. The pride of life—a craving for fame and admiration, which is vanity.

Worldly things don’t last. Desire fades eventually, but love for God endures forever. 

Many try to avoid facing the truth of this passage by making excuses or exceptions. They want to know how much they can still be worldly and love the world. But the message here is clear. 

If someone doesn’t overcome the world within themselves, they won’t grow spiritually. Without starting this battle inside, a person might fall away or just pretend to follow Jesus. 

These temptations are very strong because of the sin in us. So, we need to watch ourselves closely and pray constantly to resist the world and its ruler.

2.o: Every man is an antichrist, who denies the Person, or any of the offices of Christ; and in denying the Son, he denies the Father also, and has no part in his favour while he rejects his great salvation. Let this prophecy that seducers would rise in the Christian world, keep us from being seduced. The church knows not well who are its true members, and who are not, but thus true Christians were proved, and rendered more watchful and humble. True Christians are anointed ones; their names expresses this: they are anointed with grace, with gifts and spiritual privileges, by the Holy Spirit of grace. The great and most hurtful lies that the father of lies spreads in the world, usually are falsehoods and errors relating to the person of Christ. The unction from the Holy One, alone can keep us from delusions. While we judge favourably of all who trust in Christ as the Divine Saviour, and obey his word, and seek to live in union with them, let us pity and pray for those who deny the Godhead of Christ, or his atonement, and the new-creating work of the Holy Ghost. Let us protest against such antichristian doctrine, and keep from them as much as we may.
2.m:
Everyone who denies Jesus or any of his roles is an antichrist. By rejecting the Son, you reject the Father too and miss out on God’s favor if you refuse his salvation.

Remember this warning about false teachers rising up in the Christian world. We need to stay alert so we aren’t fooled. 

The church often doesn't know which people are truly believers. But testing helps real Christians prove themselves and become more careful and humble.

True Christians are called “anointed ones” because God sets them apart with his grace, gifts, and blessings through the Holy Spirit.

Satan spreads many harmful lies, especially about Jesus’ nature. Only the Holy Spirit can protect us from these tricks.

While we should welcome everyone who trusts in Jesus as Savior, obeys his teachings, and tries to live united with other believers, we should also feel sorry for those who deny Jesus' divinity or his work on the cross. And we should pray for them.

We must speak out against false beliefs and avoid those who teach them whenever possible.

2.o: The truth of Christ, abiding in us, is a means to sever from sin, and unites us to the Son of God, Joh 15:3, 4. What value should we put upon gospel truth! Thereby the promise of eternal life is made sure. The promise God makes, is suitable to his own greatness, power, and goodness; it is eternal life. The Spirit of truth will not lie; and he teaches all things in the present dispensation, all things necessary to our knowledge of God in Christ, and their glory in the gospel. The apostle repeats the kind words, “little children;” which denotes his affection. He would persuade by love. Gospel privileges oblige to gospel duties; and those anointed by the Lord Jesus abide with him. The new spiritual nature is from the Lord Christ. He that is constant to the practice of religion in trying times, shows that he is born from above, from the Lord Christ. Then, let us beware of holding the truth in unrighteousness, remembering that those only are born of God, who bear his holy image, and walk in his most righteous ways.
2.m:
Christ living in us helps us leave sin behind and connects us to the Son of God, John 15:3, 4. How valuable is the truth of the gospel! It makes the promise of eternal life certain. God’s promise fits his greatness, power, and kindness. It gives us eternal life. The Holy Spirit doesn’t lie. He teaches us everything we need to know about God through Jesus in the gospel. 

The apostle calls them “little children,” showing his care for them. He wants to convince them with love. Having gospel rights comes with doing gospel duties. Those who are anointed by Jesus stay close to him. Our new spiritual nature comes from Jesus. Someone who keeps practicing religion even during hard times proves they were born again, from Jesus. 

Let’s be careful not to hold onto truth while being unfair. Remember, only those who show God’s holy image and follow his right ways are truly born of God.

3.o: Little does the world know of the happiness of the real followers of Christ. Little does the world think that these poor, humble, despised ones, are favourites of God, and will dwell in heaven. Let the followers of Christ be content with hard fare here, since they are in a land of strangers, where their Lord was so badly treated before them. The sons of God must walk by faith, and live by hope. They may well wait in faith, hope, and earnest desire, for the revelation of the Lord Jesus. The sons of God will be known, and be made manifest by likeness to their Head. They shall be transformed into the same image, by their view of him.
3.m:
The world doesn't realize how happy true followers of Christ really are. People don’t know that these humble, often ignored believers are God’s favorites and will one day live in heaven. Followers of Christ should be okay with simple food here because they’re living in a foreign place, just like their Lord did before them. 

Christians need to trust in what they believe and look forward to seeing Jesus. They can patiently wait in faith and hope for his return. True Christians will be recognized by how much they resemble their leader, Jesus. When he appears, they will be changed to look like him.

3.o: The sons of God know that their Lord is of purer eyes than to allow any thing unholy and impure to dwell with him. It is the hope of hypocrites, not of the sons of God, that makes allowance for gratifying impure desires and lusts. May we be followers of him as his dear children, thus show our sense of his unspeakable mercy, and express that obedient, grateful, humble mind which becomes us. Sin is the rejecting the Divine law. In him, that is, in Christ, was no sin. All the sinless weaknesses that were consequences of the fall, he took; that is, all those infirmities of mind or body which subject man to suffering, and expose him to temptation. But our moral infirmities, our proneness to sin, he had not. He that abides in Christ, continues not in the practice of sin. Renouncing sin is the great proof of spiritual union with, continuance in, and saving knowledge of the Lord Christ. Beware of self-deceit. He that doeth righteousness is righteous, and to be a follower of Christ, shows an interest by faith in his obedience and sufferings. But a man cannot act like the devil, and at the same time be a disciple of Christ Jesus. Let us not serve or indulge what the Son of God came to destroy. To be born of God is to be inwardly renewed by the power of the Spirit of God. Renewing grace is an abiding principle. Religion is not an art, a matter of dexterity and skill, but a new nature. And the regenerate person cannot sin as he did before he was born of God, and as others do who are not born again. There is that light in his mind, which shows him the evil and malignity of sin. There is that bias upon his heart, which disposes him to loathe and hate sin. There is the spiritual principle that opposes sinful acts. And there is repentance for sin, if committed. It goes against him to sin with forethought. The children of God and the children of the devil have their distinct characters. The seed of the serpent are known by neglect of religion, and by their hating real Christians. He only is righteous before God, as a justified believer, who is taught and disposed to righteousness by the Holy Spirit. In this the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil. May all professors of the gospel lay these truths to heart, and try themselves by them.
3.m:
The sons of God know that their Lord won’t let anything unholy stay with him. Hypocrites might think differently, but true believers don’t allow themselves to give in to wrong desires. We should follow him as his children and show our gratitude for his love. Sin means breaking God’s laws. Christ had no sin. He took on all the problems caused by the fall—like sickness and pain—but he didn’t have a tendency toward sin himself. Those who stay close to Christ don’t keep doing wrong things. Not sinning proves you’re truly connected to him. Be careful not to fool yourself. Doing good shows you believe in Christ and share in his life and work. You can’t follow both Christ and the devil. Don’t do what Christ came to end. Being born of God means having your heart changed by the Spirit. This change stays with you. True faith isn’t just about skills—it’s about having a new way of thinking. People who are truly changed by God can’t live the same old sinful ways anymore. They see the badness of sin clearly now. Their hearts turn away from it. They feel sorry if they do sin. It hurts them to plan to do wrong things. God sees people as righteous if they listen to the Holy Spirit and try to live right.

Here the children of God are shown, and the children of the devil are shown too. Let everyone who claims to follow the gospel think about these truths and check themselves against them.

3.o: We should love the Lord Jesus, value his love, and therefore love all our brethren in Christ. This love is the special fruit of our faith, and a certain sign of our being born again. But none who rightly know the heart of man, can wonder at the contempt and enmity of ungodly people against the children of God. We know that we are passed from death to life: we may know it by the evidences of our faith in Christ, of which love to our brethren is one. It is not zeal for a party in the common religion, or affection for those who are of the same name and sentiments with ourselves. The life of grace in the heart of a regenerate person, is the beginning and first principle of a life of glory, whereof they must be destitute who hate their brother in their hearts.
3.m:
We should love Jesus and value His love. Because of this, we should also love all our brothers and sisters in Christ. Loving others is a sign that we have been changed by faith. 

People who truly understand human nature shouldn’t be surprised if ungodly people dislike believers. We know we’ve moved from spiritual death to life because of the signs of faith, such as loving other Christians.

This isn't just about supporting a religious group or liking those who think like us. Having God’s grace in our hearts marks the start of a new life that leads to eternal glory. Those who hate their Christian brothers and sisters miss out on this.

3.o: Here is the condescension, the miracle, the mystery of Divine love, that God would redeem the church with his own blood. Surely we should love those whom God has loved, and so loved. The Holy Spirit, grieved at selfishness, will leave the selfish heart without comfort, and full of darkness and terror. By what can it be known that a man has a true sense of the love of Christ for perishing sinners, or that the love of God has been planted in his heart by the Holy Spirit, if the love of the world and its good overcomes the feelings of compassion to a perishing brother? Every instance of this selfishness must weaken the evidences of a man's conversion; when habitual and allowed, it must decide against him. If conscience condemn us in known sin, or the neglect of known duty, God does so too. Let conscience therefore be well-informed, be heard, and diligently attended to.
3.m:
God showed great love by saving the church with his own blood. We should love others who God loves. When people are only thinking about themselves, the Holy Spirit leaves them feeling sad and scared. 

How do we know someone truly feels the love Jesus had for lost sinners? Someone who cares more about worldly things than helping their fellow humans probably doesn't have that kind of love. Each time someone acts selfishly, it weakens signs of their real change. If they keep being selfish, it proves they aren't really converted.

If our conscience tells us we've sinned or neglected duties, then God agrees with that. So listen carefully to your conscience and follow its guidance.

3.o: When believers had confidence towards God, through the Spirit of adoption, and by faith in the great High Priest, they might ask what they would of their reconciled Father. They would receive it, if good for them. And as good-will to men was proclaimed from heaven, so good-will to men, particularly to the brethren, must be in the hearts of those who go to God and heaven. He who thus follows Christ, dwells in Him as his ark, refuge, and rest, and in the Father through him. This union between Christ and the souls of believers, is by the Spirit he has given them. A man may believe that God is gracious before he knows it; yet when faith has laid hold on the promises, it sets reason to work. This Spirit of God works a change; in all true Christians it changes from the power of Satan to the power of God. Consider, believer, how it changes thy heart. Dost not thou long for peace with God? Wouldst thou not forego all the world for it? No profit, pleasure, or preferment shall hinder thee from following Christ. This salvation is built upon Divine testimony, even the Spirit of God.
3.m:
When believers trust in God through the Holy Spirit and have faith in Jesus, their great High Priest, they can ask anything from their loving Father. Their requests will be granted if it’s best for them. Just as kindness comes down from heaven, believers should show kindness to each other. 

Those who follow Christ live inside him, just like someone living safely in an ark. The connection between Christ and the lives of believers happens because of the Holy Spirit within them. People can believe God is kind without knowing much about him first. But once faith holds onto God’s promises, it makes sense to us.

The Holy Spirit brings real change. In true followers of Christ, this change moves them from being controlled by evil forces to being guided by God. Think about how this change affects you. Don’t you want peace with God more than anything else? Wouldn’t you give up everything to have it?

Nothing stops believers from following Christ—no money, fun, or success gets in the way. This salvation depends on what God’s Spirit tells us.

4.o: Christians who are well acquainted with the Scriptures, may, in humble dependence on Divine teaching, discern those who set forth doctrines according to the apostles, and those who contradict them. The sum of revealed religion is in the doctrine concerning Christ, his person and office. The false teachers spake of the world according to its maxims and tastes, so as not to offend carnal men. The world approved them, they made rapid progress, and had many followers such as themselves; the world will love its own, and its own will love it. The true doctrine as to the Saviour's person, as leading men from the world to God, is a mark of the spirit of truth in opposition to the spirit of error. The more pure and holy any doctrine is, the more likely to be of God; nor can we by any other rules try the spirits whether they are of God or not. And what wonder is it, that people of a worldly spirit should cleave to those who are like themselves, and suit their schemes and discourses to their corrupt taste?
4.m:
Christians who know the Bible well can tell if someone teaches the right things based on what the apostles taught. They can spot those who agree with the apostles and those who disagree.

The main part of the Christian faith is about Jesus—his life and role. False teachers talked about the world using ideas that pleased sinful people. 

These false teachers were popular because the world liked them. Many people followed them because they shared similar beliefs. The world loves its own kind, and those kinds love each other back.

True teachings about Jesus help people leave the world behind and turn toward God. This shows the Spirit of truth against the spirit of error. The purer and holier the teaching, the more likely it comes from God. We cannot judge spirits any other way.

It’s no surprise that people with worldly minds stick to those who think like them and talk in ways that fit their wrong desires.

4.o: The Spirit of God is the Spirit of love. He that does not love the image of God in his people, has no saving knowledge of God. For it is God's nature to be kind, and to give happiness. The law of God is love; and all would have been perfectly happy, had all obeyed it. The provision of the gospel, for the forgiveness of sin, and the salvation of sinners, consistently with God's glory and justice, shows that God is love. Mystery and darkness rest upon many things yet. God has so shown himself to be love, that we cannot come short of eternal happiness, unless through unbelief and impenitence, although strict justice would condemn us to hopeless misery, because we break our Creator's laws. None of our words or thoughts can do justice to the free, astonishing love of a holy God towards sinners, who could not profit or harm him, whom he might justly crush in a moment, and whose deserving of his vengeance was shown in the method by which they were saved, though he could by his almighty Word have created other worlds, with more perfect beings, if he had seen fit. Search we the whole universe for love in its most glorious displays? It is to be found in the person and the cross of Christ. Does love exist between God and sinners? Here was the origin, not that we loved God, but that he freely loved us. His love could not be designed to be fruitless upon us, and when its proper end and issue are gained and produced, it may be said to be perfected. So faith is perfected by its works. Thus it will appear that God dwells in us by his new-creating Spirit. A loving Christian is a perfect Christian; set him to any good duty, and he is perfect to it, he is expert at it. Love oils the wheels of his affections, and sets him on that which is helpful to his brethren. A man that goes about a business with ill will, always does it badly. That God dwells in us and we in him, were words too high for mortals to use, had not God put them before us. But how may it be known whether the testimony to this does proceed from the Holy Ghost? Those who are truly persuaded that they are the sons of God, cannot but call him Abba, Father. From love to him, they hate sin, and whatever disagrees with his will, and they have a sound and hearty desire to do his will. Such testimony is the testimony of the Holy Ghost.
4.m:
The Spirit of God is full of love. Anyone who doesn’t love the image of God in others doesn’t truly know God. Because God’s nature is to be kind and bring joy. 

God’s law is about love. Everyone would have been completely happy if everyone followed it. The gospel offers forgiveness and salvation for sinners without harming God’s honor and justice. This proves that God is love.

There are still mysteries we don’t fully understand. We won’t miss out on eternal happiness unless we doubt or refuse to repent. Even though strict justice would sentence us to endless suffering for breaking God’s rules.

None of our words can describe God’s amazing, free love toward sinners. Sinners who couldn’t help or hurt him, whom he could have destroyed instantly. Yet he saved them despite their unworthiness. He could have made other worlds with better beings if he wanted.

If we look everywhere for the greatest love, we find it in Jesus Christ. Did God first love us, even though we didn’t love him back? Yes, freely. His love wasn’t meant to be useless. When it produces its intended results, we can say it is complete. Faith becomes perfect through its actions.

This way, we see God living in us through his renewing Spirit. A loving Christian is a perfect one. Put such a person to work, and they excel. Love makes their efforts smooth and helpful to others. Someone who does tasks with bad intentions usually does them poorly.

Saying that God lives in us and we in him sounds too grand for humans to say. But God gave us these words. How can we tell if this claim comes from the Holy Spirit?

People who really believe they are God’s children naturally call him “Abba,” which means “Father.” Because they love God, they hate sin and anything against his wishes. They sincerely want to follow what he wants them to do. This kind of inner witness comes from the Holy Spirit.

4.o: The Father sent the Son, he willed his coming into this world. The apostle attests this. And whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God. This confession includes faith in the heart as the foundation; makes acknowledgment with the mouth to the glory of God and Christ, and profession in the life and conduct, against the flatteries and frowns of the world. There must be a day of universal judgment. Happy those who shall have holy boldness before the Judge at that day; knowing he is their Friend and Advocate! Happy those who have holy boldness in the prospect of that day, who look and wait for it, and for the Judge's appearance! True love to God assures believers of God's love to them. Love teaches us to suffer for him and with him; therefore we may trust that we shall also be glorified with him, 2Ti 2:12. We must distinguish between the fear of God and being afraid of him; the fear of God imports high regard and veneration for God. Obedience and good works, done from the principle of love, are not like the servile toil of one who unwillingly labours from dread of a master's anger. They are like that of a dutiful child, who does services to a beloved father, which benefit his brethren, and are done willingly. It is a sign that our love is far from perfect, when our doubts, fears, and apprehensions of God, are many. Let heaven and earth stand amazed at his love. He sent his word to invite sinners to partake of this great salvation. Let them take the comfort of the happy change wrought in them, while they give him the glory. The love of God in Christ, in the hearts of Christians from the Spirit of adoption, is the great proof of conversion. This must be tried by its effects on their temper, and their conduct to their brethren. If a man professes to love God, and yet indulges anger or revenge, or shows a selfish disposition, he gives his profession the lie. But if it is plain that our natural enmity is changed into affection and gratitude, let us bless the name of our God for this seal and earnest of eternal happiness. Then we differ from the false professors, who pretend to love God, whom they have not seen, yet hate their brethren, whom they have seen.
4.m:
The Father sent the Son into the world because he wanted him to come. The apostle confirms this. Whoever admits that Jesus is the Son of God has God living inside them, and they live in God too. This admission means believing in Jesus with all your heart, speaking about him openly, and showing your belief through your actions, even when others might try to persuade you otherwise.

There will be a final day of judgment. Those who are brave and confident before God on that day will know he is their friend and defender. People who look forward to that day with courage will be happy.

True love for God reassures believers that God loves them back. When we love God, we’re willing to suffer for him, just as he suffered for us. So we can trust that we’ll share in his glory someday, as promised in 2 Timothy 2:12.

We need to tell the difference between fearing God and being scared of him. Fearing God means having deep respect and honor for him. Doing what’s right out of love for God isn’t like working hard out of fear of punishment. Instead, it’s more like doing things happily for someone you care about deeply, such as a loving parent.

If we doubt or fear God often, it shows our love isn't strong enough. We should marvel at God’s love instead. God sends messages inviting people to join in this wonderful salvation. We should enjoy the positive changes in our lives and thank God for them.

When Christians feel God’s love through the Holy Spirit, it proves they’ve been truly converted. We can see this love in how they act and treat others. If someone claims to love God but gets angry or acts selfishly, they aren’t really loving God. But if we see that our hatred toward God has turned into love and thanks, then we know God’s love is real in our lives.

We are different from those false believers who claim to love God, even though they have never seen him. But they hate their brothers and sisters, whom they have actually seen.

5.o: True love for the people of God, may be distinguished from natural kindness or party attachments, by its being united with the love of God, and obedience to his commands. The same Holy Spirit that taught the love, will have taught obedience also; and that man cannot truly love the children of God, who, by habit, commits sin or neglects known duty. As God's commands are holy, just, and good rules of liberty and happiness, so those who are born of God and love him, do not count them grievous, but lament that they cannot serve him more perfectly. Self-denial is required, but true Christians have a principle which carries them above all hinderances. Though the conflict often is sharp, and the regenerate may be cast down, yet he will rise up and renew his combat with resolution. But all, except believers in Christ, are enslaved in some respect or other, to the customs, opinions, or interests of the world. Faith is the cause of victory, the means, the instrument, the spiritual armour by which we overcome. In and by faith we cleave to Christ, in contempt of, and in opposition to the world. Faith sanctifies the heart, and purifies it from those sensual lusts by which the world obtains sway and dominion over souls. It has the indwelling Spirit of grace, which is greater than he who dwells in the world. The real Christian overcomes the world by faith; he sees, in and by the life and conduct of the Lord Jesus on earth, that this world is to be renounced and overcome. He cannot be satisfied with this world, but looks beyond it, and is still tending, striving, and pressing toward heaven. We must all, after Christ's example, overcome the world, or it will overcome us to our ruin.
5.m:
True love for God’s people can be different from regular friendship or loyalty to a group because it comes with loving God and following his commands. The same Holy Spirit who teaches us to love also teaches us to obey. Someone who doesn’t follow God’s rules can’t really love God’s people.

God’s commands are good rules that help us live happy lives. People who belong to God and love him don't find these rules hard to follow. They wish they could obey even better. True Christians sometimes need to deny themselves, but their desire to please God helps them get past any obstacles.

Sometimes the battle is tough, and someone who follows God might feel discouraged. But they keep trying again and again. Non-believers are controlled by things like worldly habits, ideas, or interests. 

Faith gives us the strength to win against the world. By believing in Christ, we stick with him instead of the world. Faith cleanses our hearts from desires that pull us away from God. It brings the Holy Spirit inside us, who is stronger than anything in the world.

Real Christians beat the world through faith. They see that the world isn’t worth keeping. Instead, they look forward to heaven. Like Christ, we must overcome the world or it will defeat us and harm us forever.

5.o: We are inwardly and outwardly defiled; inwardly, by the power and pollution of sin in our nature. For our cleansing there is in and by Christ Jesus, the washing of regeneration and the renewing of the Holy Ghost. Some think that the two sacraments are here meant: baptism with water, as the outward sign of regeneration, and purifying from the pollution of sin by the Holy Spirit; and the Lord's supper, as the outward sign of the shedding Christ's blood, and the receiving him by faith for pardon and justification. Both these ways of cleansing were represented in the old ceremonial sacrifices and cleansings. This water and blood include all that is necessary to our salvation. By the water, our souls are washed and purified for heaven and the habitation of saints in light. By the blood, we are justified, reconciled, and presented righteous to God. By the blood, the curse of the law being satisfied, the purifying Spirit is obtained for the internal cleansing of our natures. The water, as well as the blood, came out of the side of the sacrificed Redeemer. He loved the church, and gave himself for it, that he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word; that he might present it to himself a glorious church, Eph 5:25–27. This was done in and by the Spirit of God, according to the Saviour's declaration. He is the Spirit of God, and cannot lie. Three had borne witness to these doctrines concerning the person and the salvation of Christ. The Father, repeatedly, by a voice from heaven declared that Jesus was his beloved Son. The Word declared that He and the Father were One, and that whoever had seen him had seen the Father. And the Holy Ghost, who descended from heaven and rested on Christ at his baptism; who had borne witness to Him by all the prophets; and gave testimony to his resurrection and mediatorial office, by the gift of miraculous powers to the apostles. But whether this passage be cited or not, the doctrine of the Trinity in Unity stands equally firm and certain. To the doctrine taught by the apostles, respecting the person and salvation of Christ, there were three testimonies. 1. The Holy Spirit. We come into the world with a corrupt, carnal disposition, which is enmity to God. This being done away by the regeneration and new-creating of souls by the Holy Spirit, is a testimony to the Saviour. 2. The water: this sets forth the Saviour's purity and purifying power. The actual and active purity and holiness of his disciples are represented by baptism. 3. The blood which he shed: and this was our ransom, this testifies for Jesus Christ; it sealed up and finished the sacrifices of the Old Testament. The benefits procured by his blood, prove that he is the Saviour of the world. No wonder if he that rejects this evidence is judged a blasphemer of the Spirit of God. These three witnesses are for one and the same purpose; they agree in one and the same thing.
5.m:
We are defiled both inside and outside. Inside, because of the power and effects of sin in us. Our cleansing comes through Jesus Christ, through the washing that brings new life and the renewal by the Holy Spirit. Some people think the two sacraments—baptism and the Lord’s Supper—are what is meant here. Baptism uses water as a symbol of new birth and purification from sin by the Holy Spirit. The Lord’s Supper represents the sacrifice of Christ’s blood and our receiving him by faith for forgiveness and acceptance with God. These ways of cleansing were shown in the old ceremonies. Water and blood cover everything needed for our salvation. Through water, our souls are cleaned and made ready for heaven. Through blood, we receive forgiveness, reconciliation, and righteousness before God. When the law’s curse is paid for by the blood, we get the Holy Spirit for inner cleansing. Both water and blood came from the side of the sacrificed Savior. He loved the church so much that he died for it, to clean and set it apart with the washing of water by the word. So he could present it as a beautiful, holy church, Ephesians 5:25–27 tells us. All of this happens by the work of the Holy Spirit, as the Savior said. The Spirit is true and cannot lie. There are three witnesses to these truths about Christ. First, the Father spoke from heaven saying Jesus was his beloved Son. Second, Jesus himself said he and the Father were one, and seeing him was like seeing the Father. Third, the Holy Spirit, who came down at Jesus’ baptism and showed he was the Messiah by giving the apostles special powers. Whether this verse mentions them or not, the truth about the Trinity remains solid. The apostles taught about Christ’s person and salvation, and there were three proofs. First, the Holy Spirit. We enter the world with a sinful nature that hates God.

This shows what the Savior did through the Holy Spirit, renewing people’s souls. 

The water represents the Savior's purity and cleansing power. Baptism shows the actual purity and holiness of his followers.

His shed blood paid our ransom. It proves Jesus is the Savior. The benefits from his blood show he saves the whole world. Anyone who rejects this proof is seen as someone who insults the Spirit of God. 

These three things—the Spirit, water, and blood—agree on the same message about Jesus.

5.o: Nothing can be more absurd than the conduct of those who doubt as to the truth of Christianity, while in the common affairs of life they do not hesitate to proceed on human testimony, and would deem any one out of his senses who declined to do so. The real Christian has seen his guilt and misery, and his need of such a Saviour. He has seen the suitableness of such a Saviour to all his spiritual wants and circumstances. He has found and felt the power of the word and doctrine of Christ, humbling, healing, quickening, and comforting his soul. He has a new disposition, and new delights, and is not the man that he formerly was. Yet he finds still a conflict with himself, with sin, with the flesh, the world, and wicked powers. But he finds such strength from faith in Christ, that he can overcome the world, and travel on towards a better. Such assurance has the gospel believer: he has a witness in himself, which puts the matter out of doubt with him, except in hours of darkness or conflict; but he cannot be argued out of his belief in the leading truths of the gospel. Here is what makes the unbeliever's sin so awful; the sin of unbelief. He gives God the lie; because he believes not the record that God gave of his Son. It is in vain for a man to plead that he believes the testimony of God in other things, while he rejects it in this. He that refuses to trust and honour Christ as the Son of God, who disdains to submit to his teaching as Prophet, to rely on his atonement and intercession as High Priest, or to obey him as King, is dead in sin, under condemnation; nor will any outward morality, learning, forms, notions, or confidences avail him.
5.m:
It’s silly for people to doubt the truth of Christianity when they believe other things based on what others tell them. A true Christian sees their sins and needs a Savior. They see Jesus as perfect for their needs. They feel the power of Jesus’ words changing them, making them humble, healed, happy, and comforted. They have new feelings and aren’t the same person they used to be. 

But they still fight against bad habits, sin, and evil forces. With faith in Jesus, though, they can win these battles and keep moving forward. This gives believers confidence in the main teachings of the gospel. Only during dark times might they doubt, but they won't give up believing.

This makes the sin of disbelief very serious. When someone doesn’t believe what God said about His Son, they’re lying to God. People can’t say they believe some parts of God’s message but reject others. Anyone who doesn’t accept Jesus as God’s Son, listen to Him as a teacher, depend on His sacrifice, or follow Him as king is trapped by sin. Good behavior, education, traditions, or beliefs won’t help them.

5.o: Upon all this evidence, it is but right that we believe on the name of the Son of God. Believers have eternal life in the covenant of the gospel. Then let us thankfully receive the record of Scripture. Always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that our labour is not in vain in the Lord. The Lord Christ invites us to come to him in all circumstances, with our supplications and requests, notwithstanding the sin that besets us. Our prayers must always be offered in submission to the will of God. In some things they are speedily answered; in others they are granted in the best manner, though not as requested. We ought to pray for others, as well as for ourselves. There are sins that war against spiritual life in the soul, and the life above. We cannot pray that the sins of the impenitent and unbelieving should, while they are such, be forgiven them; or that mercy, which supposes the forgiveness of sins, should be granted to them, while they wilfully continue such. But we may pray for their repentance, for their being enriched with faith in Christ, and thereupon for all other saving mercies. We should pray for others, as well as for ourselves, beseeching the Lord to pardon and recover the fallen, as well as to relieve the tempted and afflicted. And let us be truly thankful that no sin, of which any one truly repents, is unto death.
5.m:
We should believe in the Son of God because of all the proof we have. People who believe have eternal life through the promises of the gospel. So let’s accept what the Scriptures tell us with gratitude. Let’s keep working hard for the Lord, knowing that our efforts won’t go to waste.

Jesus invites us to come to him anytime, even if we’re struggling with sin. When we pray, we need to submit to God’s will. Sometimes he answers quickly, and sometimes he answers in the best way possible, even if not exactly as we asked.

We should pray for others too, just as we do for ourselves. Some sins fight against spiritual growth and life. We can’t ask God to forgive those who refuse to turn from their sins. But we can pray that they’ll change their minds and trust in Jesus. 

We should pray for people to have faith in Christ and all the blessings that follow. Pray for others to be forgiven and restored, just as you would for yourself. Be grateful that anyone who truly repents gets God’s forgiveness.

5.o: All mankind are divided into two parties or dominions; that which belongs to God, and that which belongs to the wicked one. True believers belong to God: they are of God, and from him, and to him, and for him; while the rest, by far the greater number, are in the power of the wicked one; they do his works, and support his cause. This general declaration includes all unbelievers, whatever their profession, station, or situation, or by whatever name they may be called. The Son leads believers to the Father, and they are in the love and favour of both; in union with both, by the indwelling and working of the Holy Spirit. Happy are those to whom it is given to know that the Son of God is come, and to have a heart to trust in and rely on him that is true! May this be our privilege; we shall thus be kept from all idols and false doctrines, and from the idolatrous love of worldly objects, and be kept by the power of God, through faith, unto eternal salvation. To this living and true God, be glory and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.
5.m:
All people are split into two groups: those who belong to God and those who belong to the devil. True believers belong to God—they come from him, live for him, and serve him. Most others follow the devil, doing what he wants them to do. 

This applies to everyone who doesn’t believe, no matter who they are or what they call themselves. The Son brings believers to the Father, so they are loved by both. They stay united with both because of the Holy Spirit’s work within them. 

It’s wonderful to know that the Son of God has come and to trust in him. When we do this, we’re protected from false beliefs and the desire for worldly things. We’re kept safe by God’s power through our faith until we reach eternal life.

To the real, living God, let there always be honor and power forever. Amen.
